task_completion.visual_evidence: ImageJ window displaying the 'blobs.gif' sample image.

task_completion.description: Create a binary mask of the objects
task_completion.visual_evidence: The blobs image is thresholded, appearing as a pure black and white binary mask.
A new image window displaying the thickness map, typically a 32-bit grayscale or color-mapped image where thicker regions of the blobs are brighter.

task_completion.description: Save the thickness map as a TIFF file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing 'thickness_map.tif' being saved to the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Analyze the thickness distribution to get summary statistics
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Results' table or 'Histogram' window in ImageJ showing statistics (Mean, StdDev, Min, Max) calculated specifically from the thickness map.

task_completion.description: Save the summary statistics as a CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing 'thickness_distribution.csv' being saved to the '~/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the ImageJ GUI to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with ImageJ menus (e.g., Image > Adjust > Threshold, Plugins > Local Thickness, Analyze > Measure) rather than just running a terminal script to generate the outputs.

integrity.description: The thickness map is genuinely derived from the blobs image
integrity.visual_evidence: The thickness map visually matches the structure of the blobs image, with pixel intensities corresponding to the width of the blobs (thicker parts are brighter).

integrity.description: The statistics are genuinely calculated from the thickness map
integrity.visual_evidence: The statistics shown in the Results window or saved CSV are consistent with the Blobs image (Mean ~8.4-8.8, Max ~30-34), not arbitrary or manually typed numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Blobs (25K)' image is a standard ImageJ sample. The expected thickness map is a 32-bit float image where values represent the diameter of the largest fitting sphere. For the Blobs image, the mean thickness should be approximately 8.4-8.8 pixels, and the maximum thickness should be 30-34 pixels. The output files must be saved to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/.
